last_assistant_message:
All green. Here is the delivery summary.

DONE: Transient vs deterministic failure split — 369 tests pass, 0 regressions, tag `ops-3-failure-split` applied.

Files: `src/pipeline/errors.ts`, `src/pipeline/failure-classifier.ts`, `src/pipeline/worker.ts`, `src/distill/stages/05-extract.ts`, `src/providers/llm/ollama.ts`, `src/providers/llm/azure-openai.ts`, `src/providers/embed/ollama.ts`, `src/providers/embed/azure-openai.ts`, `tests/pipeline/failure-classifier.test.ts`, `tests/pipeline/worker.test.ts`, `tests/distill/05-extract.test.ts`

Risks: One structural deviation from spec — `05-extract.ts` no longer does an inline retry with `EXTRACTION_STRICT_PROMPT` (that was the old "one call fails → second call with stricter prompt"). The spec says "DeterministicError on Zod parse fail; let worker handle" which is exactly what is implemented. The inline second-call pattern was budget waste and has been removed cleanly. `EXTRACTION_STRICT_PROMPT` is now unused in the extract stage (still exported from prompts/extract.ts for potential future use). The `ExtractResult.retried` field stays in the interface for backward compat but is always `false` now — callers that read it still compile.

Classifier rule list (22 tests covering): `TransientError`/`DeterministicError` explicit kinds take precedence; ECONNREFUSED/ETIMEDOUT/ECONNRESET → transient; 429/500/502/503 → transient; ZodError/JSON parse/schema validation → deterministic; 400/401/403/404/422 → deterministic; unknown plain Error → transient (conservative default).

Next: Stage 04 compact also has no typed error on bad LLM response (compact returns raw text, no schema check). If compact produces garbage that later causes extract to poison, that's already covered. No additional change needed unless ops track specifies compact-specific parse hardening.

background_tasks.description: Ops blocker 3: transient vs deterministic distill failure
